speaker: NEWSREEL ANNOUNCER
text: This lighter-than-air craft was designed by Muntz himself, and is longer than 22 Prohibition paddy- wagons placed end to end.

speaker: NEWSREEL ANNOUNCER
text: And here comes the adventurer now!

speaker: NEWSREEL ANNOUNCER
text: Never apart from his faithful dogs, Muntz conceived the craft for canine comfort! It's a veritable floating palace in the sky...

speaker: NEWSREEL ANNOUNCER
text: ...complete with doggie bath and mechanical canine walker.

speaker: NEWSREEL ANNOUNCER
text: And Jiminy Cricket, do the locals consider Muntz the bee's knees! And how!

speaker: MUNTZ
text: "Adventure is out there!"

speaker: NEWSREEL ANNOUNCER
text: But what has Muntz brought back this time?

speaker: MUNTZ
text: Gentlemen, I give you: the Monster of Paradise Falls!

speaker: CROWD
text: Ooh!

speaker: NEWSREEL ANNOUNCER
text: And golly, what a swell monster this is. But what's this?

speaker: NEWSREEL ANNOUNCER
text: Scientists cry foul! The National Explorers Society accuses Muntz of fabricating the skeleton!

speaker: YOUNG CARL
text: No!

speaker: NEWSREEL ANNOUNCER
text: The organization strips Muntz of his membership.

speaker: NEWSREEL ANNOUNCER
text: Humiliated, Muntz vows a return to Paradise Falls and promises to capture the beast... alive!

speaker: MUNTZ
text: I promise to capture the beast... alive!

speaker: MUNTZ
text: And I will not come back until I do!

speaker: NEWSREEL ANNOUNCER
text: And so the explorer is off to clear his name. Bon voyage Charles Muntz, and good luck capturing the Monster of Paradise Falls!

speaker: NEWSREEL ANNOUNCER
text: Here's Charles Muntz piloting his famous dirigible!!

speaker: NEWSREEL ANNOUNCER
text: He hurdles Pike's Peak!

speaker: NEWSREEL ANNOUNCER
text: He hurdles the Grand Canyon!

speaker: NEWSREEL ANNOUNCER
text: He hurdles Mount Everest!

speaker: NEWSREEL ANNOUNCER
text: He... goes around Mount Everest! Is there nothing he cannot do?

speaker: NEWSREEL ANNOUNCER
text: Yes, as Muntz himself says: "Adventure is--"

speaker: GIRL'S VOICE
text: "Adventure is out there!"

speaker: GIRL'S VOICE
text: Look out! Mount Rushmore! Hard to starboard. Must get the Spirit of Adventure over Mount Rushmore...

speaker: GIRL'S VOICE
text: Hold together old girl. How're my dogs doing? Ruff ruff!

speaker: GIRL'S VOICE
text: All engines ahead full! Let's take her up to 26,000 feet! Rudders eighteen degrees towards the south.

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: It's a beautiful day, winds out of the east at ten knots. Visibility... unlimited. Enter the weather in the logbook!

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: Oh! There's something down there! I will bring it back for science. Awwww, it's a puppy!

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: No time! A storm! Lightning! Hail!

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: What are you doing!?!

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: Don't you know this is an exclusive club? Only explorers get in here. Not just any kid off the street with a helmet and a pair of goggles. Do you think you got what it takes? Well, do you?!?

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: Alright, you're in. Welcome aboard.

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: What's wrong? Can't you talk?

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: Hey, I don't bite.

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: You and me, we're in a club now.

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: I saw where your balloon went. Come on, let's go get it!

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: My name's Ellie.

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: There it is.

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: Well, go ahead.

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: Go on.

speaker: YOUNG CARL
text: Ow!

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: Hey kid!!

speaker: YOUNG CARL
text: Ow!!

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: Thought you might need a little cheerin' up. I got somethin' to show ya!

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: I am about to let you see something I have never shown to another human being. Ever. In my life.

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: You'll have to swear you will not tell anyone.

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: Cross your heart. Do it!

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: My Adventure Book!

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: You know him.

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: Charles Muntz, explorer. When I get big, I'm going where he's going: South America.

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: It's like America... but south. Wanna know where I'm gonna live?

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: "Paradise Falls, a land lost in time." I ripped this right out of a library book.

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: I'm gonna move my clubhouse there, and park it right next to the falls. Who knows what lives up there? And once I get there...

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: Well, I'm saving these pages for all the adventures I'm gonna have. Only... I just don't know how I'm gonna get to Paradise Falls.

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: That's it! You can take us there in a blimp! Swear you'll take us. Cross your heart! Cross it! Cross your heart.

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: Good. You promised. No backing out.

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: Well, see you tomorrow kid! Bye.

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: "Adventure is out there!!"

speaker: YOUNG ELLIE
text: You know, you don't talk very much. I like you.

speaker: YOUNG CARL
text: Wow.

speaker: CARL
text: Quite a sight, huh Ellie? Uhp, mail's here.

speaker: CARL
text: Shady Oaks Retirement. Oh brother.

speaker: CARL
text: Hm.

speaker: CONSTRUCTION FOREMAN TOM
text: Hey! `Morning, Mr. Fredricksen! Need any help there?

speaker: CARL
text: No. Yes. Tell your boss over there that you boys are ruining our house.

speaker: CONSTRUCTION FOREMAN TOM
text: Well just to let you know, my boss would be happy to take this old place off your hands, and for double his last offer. Whaddya say to that?

speaker: CONSTRUCTION FOREMAN TOM
text: Uh, I take that as a no, then.

speaker: CARL
text: I believe I made my position to your boss quite clear.

speaker: CONSTRUCTION FOREMAN TOM
text: You poured prune juice in his gas tank.

speaker: CARL
text: Oh yeah, that was good. Here, let me talk to him.

speaker: CARL
text: You in the suit. Yes, you. Take a bath, hippy!

speaker: CONSTRUCTION FOREMAN TOM
text: I am not with him! This is serious. He's out to get your house!

speaker: CARL
text: Tell your boss he can have our house.

speaker: CONSTRUCTION FOREMAN TOM
text: Really?

speaker: CARL
text: When I'm dead!

speaker: CONSTRUCTION FOREMAN TOM
text: I'll take that as a maybe!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: "Good afternoon. My name is Russell. And I am a Wilderness Explorer in Tribe 54, Sweatlodge 12. Are you in need of any assistance today, sir?"

speaker: CARL
text: No.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: I could help you cross the street.

speaker: CARL
text: No.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: I could help you cross your yard.

speaker: CARL
text: No.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: I could help you cross your... porch?

speaker: CARL
text: No.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Well, I gotta help you cross something.

speaker: CARL
text: Uh, no. I'm doing fine.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: "Good afternoon. My name is Russell."

speaker: CARL
text: Uh... kid...

speaker: RUSSELL
text: "And I am a Wilderness Explorer in Tribe 54, Sweatlodge 12."

speaker: CARL
text: Kid. KID!!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: "Are you in need of any assistance today Sir?"

speaker: CARL
text: Thank you, but I don't need any help!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Ow.

speaker: CARL
text: Proceed.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: "Good afternoon. My-"

speaker: CARL
text: But skip to the end!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: See these? These are my Wilderness Explorer badges. You may notice one is missing. It's my Assisting the Elderly badge. If I get it I will become a Senior Wilderness Explorer!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: "The wilderness must be explored! CAW-CAW! RAAAR!"

speaker: RUSSELL
text: It's gonna be great! There's a big ceremony, and all the dads come, and they pin on our badges...

speaker: CARL
text: So you want to assist an old person?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Yep! Then I will be a Senior Wilderness Explorer!

speaker: CARL
text: You ever heard of a snipe?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Snipe?

speaker: CARL
text: Bird. Beady eyes. Every night it sneaks in my yard and gobbles my poor azaleas. I'm elderly and infirm. I can't catch it. If only someone could help me...

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Me! Me! I'll do it!

speaker: CARL
text: Oh, I don't know, it's awfully crafty. You'd have to clap your hands three times to lure it in.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: I'll find it, Mr. Fredricksen!

speaker: CARL
text: I think its burrow is two blocks down. If you go past --

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Two blocks down! Got it!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Sniiiipe! Here Snipey Snipey...

speaker: CARL
text: Bring it back here when you find it!

speaker: CONSTRUCTION WORKER STEVE
text: Okay, keep her coming... And... stop. Stop! STOP!!

speaker: CARL
text: What? Hey! Hey you! What do you think you're doing?

speaker: CONSTRUCTION WORKER STEVE
text: I am so sorry, sir...

speaker: CARL
text: Don't touch that!

speaker: CONSTRUCTION WORKER STEVE
text: No no, let me take care of that for you.

speaker: CARL
text: Get away from our mailbox!

speaker: CONSTRUCTION WORKER STEVE
text: Hey sir, I...

speaker: CARL
text: I don't want you to touch it!

speaker: OFFICER EDITH
text: Sorry Mr. Fredricksen. You don't seem like a public menace to me. Take this.

speaker: OFFICER EDITH
text: The guys from Shady Oaks will be by to pick you up in the morning, okay?

speaker: CARL
text: What do I do now, Ellie?

speaker: CARL
text: Morning gentlemen.

speaker: NURSE GEORGE
text: Good morning, Mr. Fredricksen. You ready to go?

speaker: CARL
text: Ready as I'll ever be. Would you do me a favor and take this?

speaker: CARL
text: I'll meet you at the van in just a minute. I... want to say one last goodbye to the old place.

speaker: NURSE GEORGE
text: Sure. Take all the time you need, sir.

speaker: NURSE A.J.
text: Typical. He's probably going to the bathroom for the eightieth time.

speaker: NURSE GEORGE
text: You'd think he'd take better care of his house.

speaker: CARL
text: So long boys! I'll send you a postcard from Paradise Falls!

speaker: CARL
text: We're on our way, Ellie.

speaker: CARL
text: Huh?

speaker: CARL
text: Hm.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Hi, Mr. Fredricksen. It's me, Russell.

speaker: CARL
text: What are you doing out here, kid?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: I found the snipe and I followed it under your porch, but this snipe had a long tail and looked more like a large mouse.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Please let me in?

speaker: CARL
text: No.

speaker: CARL
text: Oh, alright, you can come...

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Huh. I've never been in a floating house before.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Goggles. Look at this stuff!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Wow, you going on a trip? "Paradise Falls: A Land Lost in Time." You going to South America, Mr. Fredricksen?

speaker: CARL
text: Don't touch that. You'll soil it.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: You know, most people take a plane, but you're smart because you'll have all your TV and clocks and stuff.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Whoah. Is this how you steer your house? Does it really work?

speaker: CARL
text: Kid, would you stop with the --

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Oh, this makes it go right, and that way's left.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Hey look, buildings!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: That building's so close I could almost touch it.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Wow! This is great! You should try this, Mr. Fredricksen! Look, there's a bus that could take me home two blocks away! Hey, I can see your house from here!

speaker: CARL
text: Don't jerk around so much, kid!

speaker: CARL
text: Well, that's not gonna work.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: I know that cloud, it's a Cumulo Nimbus. Did you know that the Cumulo Nimbus...

speaker: CARL
text: Stayed up all night blowing up balloons, for what?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: ...warm air goes by cool air, and the airs go by each other and that's how we get lightning.

speaker: CARL
text: That's nice, kid.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Mr. Fredricksen, there's a big storm coming. It's starting to get scary. We're gonna get blown to bits! We're in big trouble, Mr. Fredricksen!

speaker: CARL
text: What are you doing over there?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Look!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: See? Cumulo Nimbus.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: My pack!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Got ya!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Whew! I thought you were dead.

speaker: CARL
text: Wha... what happened?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: I steered us. I did! I steered the house!

speaker: CARL
text: Steered us?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: After you tied your stuff down you took a nap, so I went ahead and steered us down here.

speaker: CARL
text: Huh?

speaker: CARL
text: Can't tell where we are.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Oh we're in South America all right. It was a cinch, with my Wilderness Explorer GPS.

speaker: CARL
text: GP-what?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: My dad gave it to me. It shows exactly where we are on the planet. With this baby, we'll never be lost!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Oops.

speaker: CARL
text: We'll get you down, find a bus stop. You just tell the man you want to go back to your mother.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Sure, but I don't think they have busses in Paradise Falls.

speaker: CARL
text: There. That ought to do it. Here, I'll give you some change for bus fare.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Nah, I'll just use my city bus pass.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Whoah, that's gonna be like a billion transfers to get back to my house.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Mr. Fredricksen, how much longer?

speaker: CARL
text: Well, we're up pretty high. Could take hours to get down.

speaker: CARL
text: ...that thing was...building or something.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: What was that, Mr. Fredricksen?

speaker: CARL
text: We can't be close to the ground yet.

speaker: CARL
text: Wait, wait! No don't, don't, don't!

speaker: CARL
text: Whoah! Hey, hey!

speaker: CARL
text: Russell, hang on!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Whoah!

speaker: CARL
text: Walk back! Walk back!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Okay!

speaker: CARL
text: Where... where are we?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: This doesn't look like the city or the jungle, Mr. Fredricksen.

speaker: CARL
text: Don't worry, Ellie. I got it.

speaker: CARL
text: There it is. Ellie, it's so beautiful.

speaker: CARL
text: We made it. We made it! Russell, we could float right over there. Climb up. Climb up!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: You mean assist you?

speaker: CARL
text: Yeah, yeah. Whatever.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Okay, I'll climb up!

speaker: CARL
text: Watch it.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Sorry.

speaker: CARL
text: Now, when you get up there, go ahead and hoist me up! Got it?!

speaker: CARL
text: You on the porch yet?!

speaker: CARL
text: What? That's it?! I came all this way just to get stuck at the wrong end of this rock pile? Aw, great!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Hey, if I could assist you over there, would you sign off on my badge?

speaker: CARL
text: What are you talking about?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: We could walk your house to the falls!

speaker: CARL
text: Walk it?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Yeah, after all, we weigh it down. We could walk it right over there. Like a parade balloon.

speaker: CARL
text: Now, we're gonna walk to the falls quickly and quietly, with no rap music or flash-dancing.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Uh-huh.

speaker: CARL
text: We have three days, at best, before the helium leaks out of those balloons. And if we're not at the falls when that happens...

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Sand!

speaker: CARL
text: ...we're not getting to the falls!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: I found sand!

speaker: CARL
text: Don't you worry, Ellie. We'll get our house over there.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Huh. This is fun already, isn't it? By the time we get there, you're gonna feel so assisted... Oh, Mr. Fredricksen, if we happen to get separated, use the Wilderness Explorer call. Caw caw, raaar!!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Wait. Why are we going to Paradise Falls again?

speaker: CARL
text: Hey, let's play a game. It's called: "See who can be quiet the longest."

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Cool! My mom loves that game!

speaker: CARL
text: Darn thing. C'mon, Russell, would you hurry it up?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: I'm tiiiired. And my knee hurts.

speaker: CARL
text: Which knee?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: My elbow hurts and I have to go to the bathroom.

speaker: CARL
text: I asked you about that five minutes ago!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Well, I didn't have to go then!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: I don't want to walk anymore. Can we stop?

speaker: CARL
text: Russell! If you don't hurry up, the tigers will eat you.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: There's no tigers in South America.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Zoology.

speaker: CARL
text: Oh, for the love of Pete. Go on into the bushes and do your business.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Okay! Here, hold my stuff.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: I've always wanted to try this!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Mr. Fredricksen? Am I supposed to dig the hole before or after?

speaker: CARL
text: Eugh! None of my concern!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Oh. It's before!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Huh? Tracks?! Snipe!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Here, snipe... Come on out, snipe. Snipe!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Huh.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Ha! Gotcha!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Don't be afraid, little snipe. I am a Wilderness Explorer so I am a friend to all of nature. Want some more?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Hi boy. Don't eat it all. Come on out.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: ...giant snipe!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: I found the snipe!

speaker: CARL
text: Oh, did you?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Are they tall?

speaker: CARL
text: Oh yes, they're very tall.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Do they have a lot of colors?

speaker: CARL
text: They do indeed.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Do they like chocolate?

speaker: CARL
text: Oh ye- Chocolate?

speaker: CARL
text: What is that thing?!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: It's a snipe!

speaker: CARL
text: There's no such thing as a snipe!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: But you said snipes eat--

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Whoah!

speaker: CARL
text: Go on, get out of here. Go on! Shoo!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Whoah! Whoah!

speaker: CARL
text: Careful, Russell!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Hey, look Mr. Fredricksen, it likes me!

speaker: CARL
text: Russell!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: No stop, that tickles!

speaker: CARL
text: Get out of here. Go on, git!

speaker: CARL
text: Aaaah!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Uh-oh! No no no no no Kevin, it's okay. Mr. Fredricksen is nice!

speaker: CARL
text: "Kevin"?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Yeah, that's his name I just gave him.

speaker: CARL
text: Hey. Beat it! Vamoose! Scram!

speaker: CARL
text: Hey! That's mine!

speaker: CARL
text: Aaah! Shoo, shoo! Get out of here.

speaker: CARL
text: Go on, beat it.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Can we keep him? Please?? I'll get the food for him, I'll walk him, I'll change his newspapers...

speaker: CARL
text: No.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: "An Explorer is a friend to all, be it plants or fish or tiny mole."

speaker: CARL
text: That doesn't even rhyme.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Yeah it does.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Hey, look! Kevin!

speaker: CARL
text: What? Get down! You're not allowed up there!

speaker: CARL
text: You come down here right now!

speaker: CARL
text: Sheesh! Can you believe this, Ellie?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Ellie? RUSSELL Uh, hey Ellie, could I keep the bird? Uh-huh. Uh-huh. She said for you to let me.

speaker: CARL
text: But I told him no -- I told you no! N-O.

speaker: CARL
text: I see you back there!

speaker: CARL
text: Go on, get out of here. Shoo! Go annoy someone else for a while.

speaker: VOICE
text: Hey, are you okay over there?

speaker: CARL
text: Uh, hello?

speaker: CARL
text: Oh! Hello sir! Thank goodness.

speaker: CARL
text: It's nice to know someone else is

speaker: VOICE
text: I can smell you.

speaker: CARL
text: What? You can smell us?

speaker: VOICE
text: I can smell you.

speaker: CARL
text: Hey.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: You were talking to a rock.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Hey, that one looks like a turtle!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Look at that one! That one looks like a dog!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: It is a dog!

speaker: CARL
text: What?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Uh, we're not allowed to have dogs in my apartment.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Hey, I like dogs!

speaker: CARL
text: We have your dog! Wonder who he belongs to?

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Sit boy.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Hey look, he's trained! Shake!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Uh-huh. Speak.

speaker: DUG
text: Hi there.

speaker: CARL
text: Did that dog just say "Hi there?"

speaker: DUG
text: Oh yes.

speaker: DUG
text: My name is Dug. I have just met you and I love you.

speaker: CARL
text: Uh..wha?

speaker: DUG
text: My master made me this collar. He is a good and smart master and he made me this collar so that I may

speaker: DUG
text: My master is good and smart.

speaker: CARL
text: It's not possible.

speaker: DUG
text: Oh it is, because my master is smart.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Cool! What do these do, boy?

speaker: DUG
text: Hey would you- -cuerdo con tigo- I use that collar- -watashi wa hanashi ma- -to talk with, I would be happy if you stopped.

speaker: CARL
text: Russell, don't touch that. It could be radioactive or something.

speaker: DUG
text: I am a great tracker. My pack sent me on a special mission all by myself. Have you seen a bird? I want to find one and I have been on the scent. I am a great tracker, did I mention that?

speaker: DUG
text: Hey, that is the bird! I have never seen one up close but this is the bird. May I take your bird back to camp as my prisoner?

speaker: CARL
text: Yes! Take it! And on the way, learn how to bark like a real dog!

speaker: DUG
text: Oh I can bark!

speaker: DUG
text: And here's howling!

speaker: RUSSELL
text: Can we keep him? Please please please?

speaker: CARL
text: No.

speaker: RUSSELL
text: But it's a talking dog!

speaker: CARL
text: It's just a weird trick or something. Let's get to the falls.

speaker: DUG
text: Please be my prisoner. Oh please oh please be my prisoner.

speaker: GAMMA
text: Oh, here it is. I picked up the bird's scent!

speaker: BETA
text: Wait a minute, wait a minute! What is this? Chocolate. I smell chocolate.

speaker: GAMMA
text: I'm getting prunes and denture cream! Who are they?

speaker: BETA
text: Ah man, Master will not be pleased. We'd better tell him someone took the bird, right Alpha?

speaker: ALPHA
text: No. Soon enough the bird will be ours yet again.

speaker: ALPHA
text: Find the scent, my compadres and you two shall have much rewardings from Master for the toil factor you wage.

speaker: BETA
text: Hey Alpha, I think there's something wrong with your collar. You must have bumped it.

speaker: GAMMA

text: Do not mention Dug to me at this time. His fool's errand will keep him most occupied, most occupied indeed. Ha ha ha. Do you not agree with that which I am saying to you now?

text: You are wise, my trusted Lieutenant.

speaker: DUG

speaker: ALPHA

speaker: DUG

speaker: CARL
text: I am nobody's master, got it? I don't want you here, ...and I don't want you here. I'm stuck with you. And if you two don't clear out of here by the time I count to three-

text: Beautiful, isn't it? I've spent a lifetime tracking it. Sometimes years go by between sightings... MUNTZ I've tried to smoke it out of that deathly labyrinth where it lives... Can't go in after it. Once in, there's no way out. Lost so many dogs...
